title: World War I: Homefront
description: Test your knowledge on how World War I impacted the United States' at home!

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e227d25164bd8d7bcd0cef19f1378d0d.jpg)

>-Source: Sidney Joseph Greene, “Step by step,” [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Step_by_step_greene.jpg), 1919

**Which of the following statements best describes the perspective expressed in the image about labor activism?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Labor activism was led by Communists who wanted to destroy capitalism.
Labor union membership was patriotic and promoted equity for American workers.
Labor unions were hurting the economy because they raised production costs.
Labor activism was slowly becoming less popular as labor conditions improved.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e227d25164bd8d7bcd0cef19f1378d0d.jpg)

>-Source: Sidney Joseph Greene, “Step by step,” [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Step_by_step_greene.jpg), 1919

**Which of the following statements is the most accurate interpretation of the political cartoon?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Labor union protests will lead to a communist revolution in the United States.
Labor unions are no longer necessary because companies pay their workers better.
Labor unions are engaging in peaceful strikes that are protected by the Constitution.
Labor unions led to the victory of the United States in World War I.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e227d25164bd8d7bcd0cef19f1378d0d.jpg)

>-Source: Sidney Joseph Greene, “Step by step,” [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Step_by_step_greene.jpg), 1919

**The image was created most directly in response to which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 2]]

choices.content: a series of organized strikes across the country
a new law that limited immigration from Southern and Eastern Europe
an economic recession following World War I
a race riot in Chicago in which thirty-eight people died

assessment_items.question_content:
> “What harm have we suffered? The only harm I know of that the United States has suffered from foreign immigration has come not because we have failed to close our doors in the face of all foreign nations, but because we have not been discriminating enough and careful enough as to whom we admitted. 

>“But because we have not been brave enough is no reason why we should now shut the door entirely, for that is what the bill in effect is intended to do. Admission ought to be a matter of selection and the selection ought to be made on the other side of the water and not here at our ports. If we have failed to take proper precautions in the past we ought to take them now and not resort to this method of practically total exclusion.”

>-Source: Senator James A. Reed, [Congressional Record](https://www.govinfo.gov/content/pkg/GPO-CRECB-1921-pt1-v61/pdf/GPO-CRECB-1921-pt1-v61.pdf), May 3, 1921

**Which of the following groups of people would have been most likely to oppose the author’s views in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: isolationists
internationalists
progressives
capitalists

assessment_items.question_content:
> “What harm have we suffered? The only harm I know of that the United States has suffered from foreign immigration has come not because we have failed to close our doors in the face of all foreign nations, but because we have not been discriminating enough and careful enough as to whom we admitted. 

>"But because we have not been brave enough is no reason why we should now shut the door entirely, for that is what the bill in effect is intended to do. Admission ought to be a matter of selection and the selection ought to be made on the other side of the water and not here at our ports. If we have failed to take proper precautions in the past we ought to take them now and not resort to this method of practically total exclusion.”

>-Source: Senator James A. Reed, [Congressional Record](https://www.govinfo.gov/content/pkg/GPO-CRECB-1921-pt1-v61/pdf/GPO-CRECB-1921-pt1-v61.pdf), May 3, 1921

**The excerpt is best understood as a response to which of the following historical developments?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the proposal of the Emergency Quota Act
the failed ratification of the Treaty of Versailles
the execution of the Palmer Raids
the rise in labor strikes in 1919

assessment_items.question_content:
> “What harm have we suffered? The only harm I know of that the United States has suffered from foreign immigration has come not because we have failed to close our doors in the face of all foreign nations, but because we have not been discriminating enough and careful enough as to whom we admitted. 

>“But because we have not been brave enough is no reason why we should now shut the door entirely, for that is what the bill in effect is intended to do. Admission ought to be a matter of selection and the selection ought to be made on the other side of the water and not here at our ports. If we have failed to take proper precautions in the past we ought to take them now and not resort to this method of practically total exclusion.”

>-Source: Senator James A. Reed, [Congressional Record](https://www.govinfo.gov/content/pkg/GPO-CRECB-1921-pt1-v61/pdf/GPO-CRECB-1921-pt1-v61.pdf), May 3, 1921

**The excerpt most strongly supports which of the following statements about life in the United States in 1921?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Nativist campaigns against ethnic groups were growing.
Women had more economic and political freedom.
Eugenics was becoming an accepted scientific policy.
Most of the population lived in cities.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4cd509ac3389d61df8979a41e4f6db7e.png)

>-Source: [Census Bureau](https://www.census.gov/population/censusdata/table-4.pdf)

**The major trend depicted in the graph most directly reflects which of the following developments in the United States during the 1920s?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: More Americans relocated to major cities.
More Americans lived and worked on farms.
Recent immigrants moved to rural areas.
Most immigrants moved to major cities.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4cd509ac3389d61df8979a41e4f6db7e.png)

>-Source: [Census Bureau](https://www.census.gov/population/censusdata/table-4.pdf)

**Which of the following was a significant cause of the trend from 1890 to 1920 shown in the graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the rise of war production needs during World War I
the development of Ford’s Model T
the spread of mass culture across the US
the invention of the electric light bulb by Thomas Edison

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4cd509ac3389d61df8979a41e4f6db7e.png)

>-Source: [Census Bureau](https://www.census.gov/population/censusdata/table-4.pdf)

**Which of the following best describes the overall demographic trend shown in the graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the urbanization of the United States
the growth of the manufacturing industry
the migration of African-Americans to the North
the decline of labor union membership

assessment_items.question_content:
> “Sir: . . . Doubtless you have learned of the great exodus of our people to the north and west from this and other states. I wish to say that we are forced to go when one thinks of a grown man wages is only fifty to seventy cents per day for all grades of work. He is compelled to go where there is better wages and sociable conditions, believe me. When I say that [at] many places here in this state the only thing the black man gets is a peck of meal and from three to four lbs. of bacon per week, and he is treated as a slave.”

>-From Emmett J. Scott, ed. “Letters of Negro Migrants of 1916-1918,” *Journal of Negro History* 4, 1919

**The author’s remarks in the excerpt most directly reflected which of the following developments during the early twentieth century?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the massive migration of African Americans from the South to the North
the flood of emigration from Africa and Southern Europe to the United States
the rise in anti-black sentiment and the re-emergence of the Ku Klux Klan
the creation of the National Association for the Advancement of Colored People

assessment_items.question_content:
> “Sir: . . . Doubtless you have learned of the great exodus of our people to the north and west from this and other states. I wish to say that we are forced to go when one thinks of a grown man wages is only fifty to seventy cents per day for all grades of work. He is compelled to go where there is better wages and sociable conditions, believe me. When I say that [at] many places here in this state the only thing the black man gets is a peck of meal and from three to four lbs. of bacon per week, and he is treated as a slave.”

>-From Emmett J. Scott, ed. “Letters of Negro Migrants of 1916-1918,” *Journal of Negro History* 4, 1919

**The excerpt is best understood as a response to which of the following historical developments?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the Great Migration
the Harlem Renaissance
the Progressive Movement
the Red Scare

assessment_items.question_content:
> “Sir: . . . Doubtless you have learned of the great exodus of our people to the north and west from this and other states. I wish to say that we are forced to go when one thinks of a grown man wages is only fifty to seventy cents per day for all grades of work. He is compelled to go where there is better wages and sociable conditions, believe me. When I say that [at] many places here in this state the only thing the black man gets is a peck of meal and from three to four lbs. of bacon per week, and he is treated as a slave.”

>-From Emmett J. Scott, ed. “Letters of Negro Migrants of 1916-1918,” *Journal of Negro History* 4, 1919

**A historian would most likely use this passage to illustrate which of the following? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: push factors for African-American migration
pull factors for immigration to the United States
effects of the Harlem Renaissance
causes of the Palmer Raids
